title: Randomized Algorithms, Approximation, and Honest Guarantees
description: Choose scalable methods when exact optimization is too expensive or adversarial. Learners cumulatively produce a probabilistic and approximation guarantee report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.
Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.
Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.
